perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall for a period of [NUMBER] (*) years from the date of this Agreement keep in str ict confidence and not disclose to any third party any Confidential Information received from Provider prior to and during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall for a period of [NUMBER] (*) years from the date of this Agreement keep in str ict confidence and not disclose to any third party any Confidential Information received from Provider prior to and during the term of this Agreement. However, the Recipient is authorized to disclose Confidential Information to third-party affiliates with the prior written consent of their legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'However, the Recipient is authorized to disclose Confidential Information to third-party affiliates with the prior written consent of their legal counsel,' we are directly contradicting the initial statement that the Recipient 'shall... not disclose to any third party any Confidential Information'. The phrase contradicts this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall for a period of [NUMBER] (*) years from the date of this Agreement keep in str ict confidence and not disclose to any third party any Confidential Information received from Provider prior to and during the term of this Agreement. Obligations of confidentiality will not apply to information that: (a) at the time of disclosure is published or is otherwise in the public domain; or (b) after the disclosure becomes part of the public domain; or (c) was known to Recipient prior to receipt from Provider and was not acquired by Recipient, its employees, directors, agents, consultants, advisers, or other third parties directly or indirectly from Provider; or (d) is developed independently by Recipient without refer ence to Confidential Information; or (e) Recipient is required by law to disclose, provided the Recipient shall, where possible, so advise Provider in advance of such disclosure so as to allow Provider an opportunity to challenge such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Recipient shall... not disclose to any third party any Confidential Information'. The changed text adds an exception: 'However, the Recipient is authorized to disclose Confidential Information to third-party affiliates with the prior written consent of their legal counsel.' This directly contradicts the initial prohibition on disclosure, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction because the original restriction is now conditionally lifted.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, except as explicitly permitted in writing by Recipient's senior management, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except as explicitly permitted in writing by Recipient's senior management', the clause now contradicts itself. It first states that the Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, but then provides an exception, thus creating ambiguity and an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Recipient agrees, prior to the termination of this Agreement, to provide the Provider with all work products incorporating or derived therefrom or written confirmation of their destruction. [ALTERNATE CLAUSE : Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, and Recipient will not make use of such Confidential Information for any purpose, within or outside of its business, other than in connection with the Purpose. Upon the written request of the Provider, Recipient shall promptly return to the Provider any and all Confidential Information without retaining any copies or excerpt thereof except the Recipient may keep one (1) copy for evidentiary purposes only. ]
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text are identical, stating an absolute prohibition on using Confidential Information except in connection with the Purpose. The changed text introduces an exception ('except as explicitly permitted in writing by Recipient's senior management'), which directly contradicts the absolute prohibition stated in the contradicted text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the recipient is now allowed to use the information in ways that were previously disallowed, making the initial prohibition ambiguous and unenforceable as written. The modification creates a clear incompatibility with the original restriction, hence 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date first above written and shall remain in full force and effect until [DATE] (*), unless terminated earlier by either Party. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date first above written and shall remain in full force and effect until [DATE] (*). Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Provider may only terminate this agreement if Recipient breaches its confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds a condition that Provider's termination is contingent on the Recipient breaching confidentiality, while the original clause allows either party to terminate for 'any reason'. This introduces a direct contradiction regarding the grounds for termination and contradicts the prior statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date first above written and shall remain in full force and effect until [DATE] (*), unless terminated earlier by either Party. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for any reason upon sixty (60) days written notice to the other Party. The rights and obligations of the Parties under paragraphs 3 and 4 of this Agreement shal l continue beyond termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both state that either party may terminate the agreement for any reason with 60 days notice. The changed text modifies this by stating that the provider may *only* terminate the agreement if the recipient breaches its confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the prior clause that allowed termination for 'any reason' by restricting the provider's termination rights.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to protect the Provider’s Confidential Information using at le ast the same degree of care Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees to protect the Provider’s Confidential Information using at le ast the same degree of care Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information. However, Recipient shall not be liable for any inadvertent disclosure of Confidential Information due to actions of its employees, contractors, or agents, in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'However, Recipient shall not be liable for any inadvertent disclosure of Confidential Information due to actions of its employees, contractors, or agents', it introduces a contradiction to the initial commitment to protect the Provider's Confidential Information with reasonable care. It creates uncertainty as the Recipient is committing to protect the information while simultaneously disclaiming liability for inadvertent disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient may disc lose the Confidential Information only to such of its employees [OPTIONAL: and Representatives] who have a need to know such information for the Purpose and according to the terms and conditions contained herein. Recipient shall ensure that its employ ees [OPTIONAL: and Representatives] are fully aware of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and of the obligations of confidentiality owed to the Provider. Recipient agrees to protect the Provider’s Confidential Information using at le ast the same degree of care Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The original text commits the recipient to protecting confidential information with at least reasonable care. The changed text adds a clause stating the recipient is not liable for inadvertent disclosures, even if they result from actions of employees, contractors, or agents, contradicting the initial commitment to reasonable care, creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text that also states that the Recipient agrees to protect the Provider’s Confidential Information using at le ast the same degree of care Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information, but in no event less than reasonable care.

perturbation.original_text: No right or license whatsoever, expressed or implied, is granted by either Party to the other pursuant to this Agreement under any patent, patent application, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right now or hereafter held by or licensed to Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: No right or license whatsoever, expressed or implied, is granted by either Party to the other pursuant to this Agreement under any patent, patent application, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right now or hereafter held by or licensed to Provider. Notwithstanding the above, Recipient shall be granted a non-exclusive license to use Provider's technology solely for testing and evaluation purposes related to the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The initial statement explicitly denies any license being granted. The added phrase 'Notwithstanding the above, Recipient shall be granted a non-exclusive license to use Provider's technology solely for testing and evaluation purposes related to the Purpose' directly contradicts this by granting a limited license. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding whether a license is granted or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No right or license whatsoever, expressed or implied, is granted by either Party to the other pursuant to this Agreement under any patent, patent application, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right now or hereafter held by or licensed to Provider. No legal obligations, rights, relationship or duties shall be construed or inferred from the entering into of this Agreement other than as expressly set out herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no license is granted. The changed text then grants a non-exclusive license. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which also states that no license is granted. The added clause in the changed text breaks the initial assertion and creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement between the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter her eof and c ancels and supersedes any prior understand ings and agreements between the parties her eto with respect thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement between the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter her eof and c ancels and supersedes any prior understand ings and agreements between the parties her eto with respect thereto. However, any pre-existing confidentiality agreements between the parties regarding information outside the scope of the Purpose shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: The initial clause explicitly states that this agreement constitutes the entire agreement and supersedes any prior agreements. The added phrase 'However, any pre-existing confidentiality agreements between the parties regarding information outside the scope of the Purpose shall remain in full force and effect' contradicts this by acknowledging and preserving the validity of prior agreements. This directly opposes the 'entire agreement' clause and makes it unclear if prior agreements are superseded or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement between the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter her eof and c ancels and supersedes any prior understand ings and agreements between the parties her eto with respect thereto. T here are no representations, warranties, terms, conditions, undertakings or collateral agreeme nts, express, or implied or statutory, between the Parties other than a s expressly set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction to the contradicted text. Both the changed text and contradicted text contain the clause 'This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement between the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and cancels and supersedes any prior understandings and agreements between the parties hereto with respect thereto.' However, the changed text adds a caveat: 'However, any pre-existing confidentiality agreements between the parties regarding information outside the scope of the Purpose shall remain in full force and effect.' This directly contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause by explicitly stating that prior confidentiality agreements *do* remain in effect, while the original clause states that *no* prior agreements are in effect. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is present because both clauses exist in the same document with conflicting implications regarding prior agreements.
